# Concepts

{{ PyPI version }} {{ License }} {{ Supported Python }} {{ Format }}

{{ Travis }} {{ Codecov }} {{ Readthedocs-stable }} {{ Readthedocs-latest }}

Concepts is a simple Python implementation of **Formal Concept Analysis**
([FCA]).

FCA provides a mathematical model for describing a set of **objects** (e.g. King
Arthur, Sir Robin, and the holy grail) with a set of **properties** (e.g. human,
knight, king, and mysterious) which each of the objects either has or not. A
table called *formal context* defines which objects have a given property and
vice versa which properties a given object has.

## Links

- GitHub: <https://github.com/xflr6/concepts>
- PyPI: <https://pypi.org/project/concepts/>
- Documentation: <https://concepts.readthedocs.io>
- Changelog: <https://concepts.readthedocs.io/en/latest/changelog.html>
- Issue Tracker: <https://github.com/xflr6/concepts/issues>
- Download: <https://pypi.org/project/concepts/#files>

## Installation

This package runs under Python 2.7 and 3.5+, use [pip] to install:

```bash
$ pip install concepts
```

This will also install the [bitsets] and [graphviz] packages from PyPI as
required dependencies.

Rendering lattice graphs depends on the [Graphviz software]. Make sure its
`dot` executable is on your systems' path.

## Quickstart

Create a **formal context** defining which object has which property, e.g. from
a simple ASCII-art style cross-table with *object* rows and *property* columns
(alternatively load a CXT or CSV file):

```python
>>> from concepts import Context

>>> c = Context.fromstring('''
...            |human|knight|king |mysterious|
... King Arthur|  X  |  X   |  X  |          |
... Sir Robin  |  X  |  X   |     |          |
... holy grail |     |      |     |     X    |
... ''')
>>> c  # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
<Context object mapping 3 objects to 4 properties [dae7402a] at 0x...>
```

Query **common properties** of objects or **common objects** of properties
(*derivation*):

```python
>>> c.intension(['King Arthur', 'Sir Robin'])
('human', 'knight')

>>> c.extension(['knight', 'mysterious'])
()
```

Get the closest matching **objects-properties pair** of objects or properties
(*formal concepts*):

```python
>>> c['Sir Robin', 'holy grail']
(('King Arthur', 'Sir Robin', 'holy grail'), ())

>>> c['king',]
(('King Arthur',), ('human', 'knight', 'king'))
```

Iterate over the **concept lattice** of all objects-properties pairs:

```python
>>> for extent, intent in c.lattice:
...     print('%r %r' % (extent, intent))
() ('human', 'knight', 'king', 'mysterious')
('King Arthur',) ('human', 'knight', 'king')
('holy grail',) ('mysterious',)
('King Arthur', 'Sir Robin') ('human', 'knight')
('King Arthur', 'Sir Robin', 'holy grail') ()
```

Make a Graphviz visualization of the lattice (use `.graphviz(view=True)` to
directly render it and display the resulting PDF):

```python
>>> c.lattice.graphviz()  # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
<graphviz.dot.Digraph object at 0x...>
```

```{image} https://raw.github.com/xflr6/concepts/master/docs/holy-grail.png
:align: center
```

## Further reading

- <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Formal_concept_analysis>
- <http://www.upriss.org.uk/fca/>

The generation of the concept lattice is based on the algorithm from C. Lindig.
[Fast Concept Analysis]. In Gerhard Stumme, editors, Working with Conceptual
Structures - Contributions to ICCS 2000, Shaker Verlag, Aachen, Germany, 2000.

The included example `CXT` files are taken from Uta Priss' [FCA homepage]

## See also

The implementation is based on these Python packages:

- [bitsets] {{ -- }} Ordered subsets over a predefined domain
- [graphviz] {{ -- }} Simple Python interface for Graphviz

The following package is build on top of concepts:

- [features] {{ -- }} Feature set algebra for linguistics

If you want to apply FCA to bigger data sets, you might want to consider [other
implementations][other implementations] based on [more sophisticated algorithms] like [In-Close]
or [Fcbo].

## License

Concepts is distributed under the [MIT license].

[bitsets]: https://pypi.org/project/bitsets/
[fast concept analysis]: http://citeseerx.ist.psu.edu/viewdoc/summary?doi=10.1.1.143.948

[fca homepage]: http://www.upriss.org.uk/fca/examples.html

modules.name: concepts.junctors
documentation:
Logical relations between sequences of truth conditions.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Template:Logical_connectives_table_and_Hasse_diagram

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Logical_connectives_Hasse_diagram.svg
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Logical_connectives_table.svg
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Logic_matrix;_operations.svg
junctors.py - logical relations between contingent boolean vectors

classes.name: concepts.junctors.Relations

methods.name: concepts.junctors.Relations.__init__
documentation: Filter out items with tautological or contradictory booleans.

Logical relations between items from their contingent truth condition sequences.

```pycon
>>> Relations(['+1', '-2 -3'], [(True, False, False), (True, False, False)])
[<Equivalent('+1', '-2 -3')>]
```

```pycon
>>> Relations(['+1', '-1'], [(True, False, False), (False, True, True)])
[<Complement('+1', '-1')>]
```

```pycon
>>> Relations(['+1', '+3'], [(True, False, False), (False, False, True)])
[<Incompatible('+1', '+3')>]
```

```pycon
>>> Relations(['+1', '-3'], [(True, False, False), (True, True, False)])
[<Implication('+1', '-3')>]
```

```pycon
>>> Relations(['-1', '-3'], [(False, True, True), (True, True, False)])
[<Subcontrary('-1', '-3')>]
```

```pycon
>>> Relations(['+1', 'sg'], [(True, True, False, False), (True, False, True, False)])
[<Orthogonal('+1', 'sg')>]
```

```pycon
>>> r = Relations(['Never', 'Always', 'Possibly', 'Maybe'],
...     [(False, False), (True, True), (True, False), (True, False)],
...     include_unary=True)
```

```pycon
>>> r  # doctest: +NORMALIZE_WHITESPACE
[<Contradiction('Never')>, <Tautology('Always')>,
 <Contingency('Possibly')>, <Contingency('Maybe')>,
 <Equivalent('Possibly', 'Maybe')>]
```

```pycon
>>> print(r)  # noqa: W291
Never    contradiction
Always   tautology
Possibly contingency
Maybe    contingency
Possibly equivalent   Maybe
```

```pycon
>>> print(r[0])
Never contradiction
```

```pycon
>>> print(r[-1])
Possibly equivalent Maybe
```

classes.name: concepts.junctors.RelationMeta
documentation: Build and retrieve conrete `Relation` subclasses from docstring tables.

classes.name: concepts.junctors.Relation
documentation: Logical characteristics of truth condition sequences.

classes.name: concepts.junctors.Unary
documentation:
Logical property of a single truth condition sequence.

                  |T|F|
Contingency   ~  0|X|X|
Contradiction t -2| |X|
Tautology     f -1|X| |

classes.name: concepts.junctors.Binary
documentation:
Logical relation between two contingent truth condition sequences.

                  |TT|TF|FT|FF|
Orthogonal   ~   7| X| X| X| X|
Subcontrary  v   6| X| X| X|  |
Implication  ->  4| X|  | X| X|
Replication  <-  5| X| X|  | X|
Equivalent   <-> 1| X|  |  | X|
Incompatible !   3|  | X| X| X|
Complement   >-< 2|  | X| X|  |

modules.name: concepts.tools
documentation: tools.py - generic helpers

functions.name: concepts.tools.max_len
documentation:
Return the len() of the longest item in `iterable` or `minimum`.

```pycon
>>> max_len(['spam', 'ham'])
4
```

```pycon
>>> max_len([])
0
```

```pycon
>>> max_len(['ham'], 4)
4
```

functions.name: concepts.tools.maximal
documentation:
Yield the unique maximal elements from `iterable` using `comparison`.

```pycon
>>> list(maximal([1, 2, 3, 3]))
[3]
```

```pycon
>>> list(maximal([1]))
[1]
```

functions.name: concepts.tools.crc32_hex
documentation:
Return unsigned CRC32 of binary data as hex-encoded string.

```pycon
>>> crc32_hex(b'spam')
'43daff3d'
```

functions.name: concepts.tools.dump_json
documentation: Serialize `obj` via {func}`json.load` to path or file-like object.

functions.name: concepts.tools.load_json
documentation: Return deserialized {func}`json.load` from path or file-like object.

classes.name: concepts.tools.Unique

methods.name: concepts.tools.Unique.replace
documentation:
Replace an item preserving order.

```pycon
>>> u = Unique([0, 1, 2])
>>> u.replace(1, 'spam')
>>> u
Unique([0, 'spam', 2])
```

```pycon
>>> u.replace('eggs', 1)
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
ValueError: 'eggs' is not in list
```

```pycon
>>> u.replace('spam', 0)
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
ValueError: 0 already in list
```

methods.name: concepts.tools.Unique.move
documentation:
Move an item to the given position.

```pycon
>>> u = Unique(['spam', 'eggs'])
>>> u.move('spam', 1)
>>> u
Unique(['eggs', 'spam'])
```

```pycon
>>> u.move('ham', 0)
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
ValueError: 'ham' is not in list
```

methods.name: concepts.tools.Unique.issuperset
documentation:
Return whether this collection contains all items.

```pycon
>>> Unique(['spam', 'eggs']).issuperset(['spam', 'spam', 'spam'])
True
```

methods.name: concepts.tools.Unique.rsub
documentation:
Return order preserving unique items not in this collection.

```pycon
>>> Unique(['spam']).rsub(['ham', 'spam', 'eggs'])
Unique(['ham', 'eggs'])
```

Unique items preserving order.

```pycon
>>> Unique([3, 2, 1, 3, 2, 1, 0])
Unique([3, 2, 1, 0])
```

classes.name: concepts.tools.lazyproperty
documentation:
Non-data descriptor caching the computed result as instance attribute.

```pycon
>>> class Spam(object):
...     @lazyproperty
...     def eggs(self):
...         return 'spamspamspam'
```

```pycon
>>> spam=Spam(); spam.eggs
'spamspamspam'
```

```pycon
>>> spam.eggs='eggseggseggs'; spam.eggs
'eggseggseggs'
```

```pycon
>>> Spam().eggs
'spamspamspam'
```

```pycon
>>> Spam.eggs  # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
<...lazyproperty object at 0x...>
```

modules.name: concepts
documentation: Formal Concept Analysis (FCA) with Python.
concepts - implement basic formal concept analysis

functions.name: concepts.load
documentation:
Load and return formal context from file.

Args:

: filename: Path to the file to load the context from.
  encoding (str): Encoding of the file (`'utf-8'`, `'latin1'`, `'ascii'`, ...).
  frmat (str): Format of the file (`'table'`, `'cxt'`, `'csv'`).

  > If `None` (default), infer `frmat` from `filename` suffix.

Returns:

: Context: New {class}`.Context` instance.

Example:
: ```pycon
  >>> load('examples/liveinwater.txt')  # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
  <Context object mapping 8 objects to 9 properties [b1e86589] at 0x...>
  ```

functions.name: concepts.load_cxt
documentation:
Load and return formal context from CXT file.

Args:

: filename: Path to the CXT file to load the context from.
  encoding (str): Encoding of the file (`'utf-8'`, `'latin1'`, `'ascii'`, ...).

Returns:

: Context: New {class}`.Context` instance.

Example:
: ```pycon
  >>> load_cxt('examples/digits.cxt')  # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
  <Context object mapping 10 objects to 7 properties [51e571e6] at 0x...>
  ```

functions.name: concepts.load_csv
documentation:
Load and return formal context from CSV file.

Args:

: filename: Path to the CSV file to load the context from.
  dialect: Syntax variant of the CSV file (`'excel'`, `'excel-tab'`).
  encoding (str): Encoding of the file (`'utf-8'`, `'latin1'`, `'ascii'`, ...).

Returns:

: Context: New {class}`.Context` instance.

Example:
: ```pycon
  >>> load_csv('examples/vowels.csv')  # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
  <Context object mapping 12 objects to 8 properties [a717eee4] at 0x...>
  ```

functions.name: concepts.make_context
documentation:
Return a new context from source string in the given format.

Args:

: source (str): Formal context table as plain-text string.
  frmat (str): Format of the context string (`'table'`, `'cxt'`, `'csv'`).

Returns:

: Context: New {class}`.Context` instance.

Example:
: ```pycon
  >>> make_context('''
  ...      |male|female|adult|child|
  ... man  |  X |      |  X  |     |
  ... woman|    |   X  |  X  |     |
  ... boy  |  X |      |     |  X  |
  ... girl |    |   X  |     |  X  |
  ... ''')  # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
  <Context object mapping 4 objects to 4 properties [65aa9782] at 0x...>
  ```

modules.name: concepts.matrices
documentation: Boolean matrices as collections of row and column vectors.
matrices.py - boolean matrices as row bitsets and column bitsets

variables.name: concepts.matrices.Vector
documentation: Single row or column of a boolean matrix as bit vector.

classes.name: concepts.matrices.Vectors
documentation: Paired collection of rows or columns of a boolean matrix relation.

classes.name: concepts.matrices.Relation
documentation:
Binary relation as interconnected pair of bitset collections.

```pycon
>>> br = Relation('Condition', 'Symbol',
... ('TT', 'TF', 'FT', 'FF'), ('->', '<-'),
... [(True, False, True, True), (True, True, False, True)])
```

```pycon
>>> br
<Relation(ConditionVectors('1011', '1101'), SymbolVectors('11', '01', '10', '11'))>
```

```pycon
>>> br[1].BitSet.frommembers(('->', '<-')).prime().members()
('TT', 'FF')
```

modules.name: concepts.contexts
documentation: Formal Concept Analysis contexts.
contexts.py - create FCA context and provide derivation and neighbors

classes.name: concepts.contexts.Context

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context.fromstring
documentation:
Return a new context from string `source` in given format.

Args:

: source (str): Formal context table as plain-text string.
  frmat (str): Format of the context string (`'table'`, `'cxt'`, `'csv'`).

Returns:

: Context: New {class}`.Context` instance.

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context.fromfile
documentation:
Return a new context from file source in given format.

Args:

: filename: Path to the file to load the context from.
  encoding (str): Encoding of the file (`'utf-8'`, `'latin1'`, `'ascii'`, ...).
  frmat (str): Format of the file (`'table'`, `'cxt'`, `'csv'`).

  > If `None` (default), infer `frmat` from `filename` suffix.

Returns:

: Context: New {class}`.Context` instance.

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context.fromjson
documentation:
Return a new context from json path or file-like object.

Args:

: path_or_fileobj: {obj}`str`, {class}`os.PathLike`, or file-like object open for reading.
  encoding (str): Ignored for file-like objects under Python 3.
  ignore_lattice (bool): Don't load lattice from json serialization.
  require_lattice (bool): Raise if no lattice in json serialization.
  raw (bool): If set, sort so the input sequences can be in any order.

  > If unset (default), assume input is already ordered for speedup

Returns:

: Context: New {class}`.Context` instance.

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context.fromdict
documentation:
Return a new context from dict `d`.

Args:

: d (dict): serialized context with optional `'lattice'`
  ignore_lattice (bool): don't load lattice from `d`
  require_lattice (bool): raise if no lattice in `d`
  raw (bool): If set, sort so the input sequences can be in any order.

  > If unset (default), assume input is already ordered for speedup

Returns:

: Context: New {class}`.Context` instance.

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context.__init__
documentation:
Create context from `objects`, `properties`, and `bools` correspondence.

Args:

: objects: Iterable of object label strings.
  properties: Iterable of property label strings.
  bools: Iterable of `len(objects)` tuples of `len(properties)` booleans.

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context.__getstate__
documentation:
Pickle context as `(intents, extents)` tuple.

Returns:

: tuple\[tuple[str, ...], tuple[str, ...]\]: Pair of `intents` and `extents`.

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context.__setstate__
documentation:
Unpickle context from `(intents, extents)` tuple.

Args:

: state (tuple\[tuple[str, ...], tuple[str, ...]\]): Pair of `intents` and `extents`.

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context.__eq__
documentation:
Return whether two contexts are equivalent.

Args:

: other (Context): Another {class}`.Context` instance.

Returns:

: bool: `True` if the contexts are equal, `False` otherwise.

Ignores `self.lattice` and `other.lattice` objects.

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context.__ne__
documentation:
Return whether two contexts are inequivalent.

Args:

: other (Context): Another {class}`.Context` instance.

Returns:

: bool: `True` if the contexts are unequal, `False` otherwise.

Ignores `self.lattice` and `other.lattice` objects.

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context._minimal
documentation: Return short lexicograpically minimum intent generating extent.

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context._minimize
documentation: Yield short lexicograpically ordered extent generating intents.

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context._neighbors
documentation:
Yield upper neighbors from extent (in colex order?).

cf. C. Lindig. 2000. Fast Concept Analysis.

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context._lattice
documentation:
Yield `(extent, indent, upper, lower)` in short lexicographic order.

cf. C. Lindig. 2000. Fast Concept Analysis.

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context.intension
documentation:
Return all properties shared by the given `objects`.

Args:

: objects: Iterable of {obj}`str` labels taken from `self.objects`.
  raw (bool): Return raw intent instead of {obj}`str` tuple.

Returns:

: tuple\[str, ...\]: A tuple of {obj}`str` labels taken from `self.properties`.

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context.extension
documentation:
Return all objects sharing the given `properties`.

Args:

: properties: Iterable of {obj}`str` labels taken from `self.properties`.
  raw (bool): Return raw extent instead of {obj}`str` tuple.

Returns:

: tuple\[str, ...\]: A tuple of {obj}`str` labels taken from `self.objects`.

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context.neighbors
documentation:
Return the upper neighbors of the concept having all given `objects`.

Args:

: objects: Iterable of {obj}`str` labels taken from `self.objects`.
  raw (bool): Return raw `(extent, intent)` pairs instead of {obj}`str` tuples.

Returns:

: list\[tuple\[tuple[str, ...], tuple[str, ...]\]: A list of upper neighbor concepts as `(extent, intent)` pairs.

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context.__getitem__
documentation:
Return `(extension, intension)` pair by shared objects or properties.

Args:

: items: Iterable of {obj}`str` labels either taken from `self.objects` or from `self.properties`.
  raw (bool): Return raw `(extent, intent)` pair instead of {obj}`str` tuples.

Returns:

: tuple\[tuple[str, ...], tuple[str, ...]\]: The smallest concept having all `items` as `(extent, intent)` pair.

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context.todict
documentation:
Return serialized context with optional lattice.

Args:
: ingnore_lattice (bool): Omit `'lattice'` in result.

  : If `None`, `'lattice'` is omitted if it has not
    yet been computed.

Returns:

: dict: A new {obj}`dict` with the serialized context.

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context.tojson
documentation:
Write serialized context as json to path or file-like object.

Args:

: path_or_fileobj: {obj}`str`, {class}`os.PathLike`, or file-like object open for writing.
  encoding (str): Ignored for file-like objects under Python 3.
  indent (int): {func}`json.dump` `indent` for pretty-printing.
  sort_keys (bool): {func}`json.dump` `sort_keys` for diffability.
  ingnore_lattice (bool): Omit `'lattice'` in result.

  > If `None`, `'lattice'` is omitted if it has not
  > yet been computed.

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context.tostring
documentation:
Return the context serialized in the given string-based format.

Args:

: frmat (str): Format of the string (`'table'`, `'cxt'`, `'csv'`).

Returns:

: str: The context as seralized string.

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context.tofile
documentation:
Save the context serialized to file in the given format.

Args:

: frmat (str): Format of the string (`'table'`, `'cxt'`, `'csv'`).
  encoding (str): Encoding of the file (`'utf-8'`, `'latin1'`, `'ascii'`, ...).

Returns:
    Relations:

methods.name: concepts.contexts.Context.lattice
documentation: Lattice: The concept lattice of the formal context.

Formal context defining a relation between objects and properties.

Create context from `objects`, `properties`, and `bools` correspondence.

Args:

: objects: Iterable of object label strings.
  properties: Iterable of property label strings.
  bools: Iterable of `len(objects)` tuples of `len(properties)` booleans.

Returns:

: Context: New {class}`.Context` instance.

Example:
: ```pycon
  >>> Context(['man', 'woman'], ['male', 'female'], [(True, False), (False, True)])  # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
  <Context object mapping 2 objects to 2 properties [47e29724] at 0x...>
  ```

Usage:

```pycon
>>> c = Context.fromstring('''
...    |+1|-1|+2|-2|+3|-3|+sg|+pl|-sg|-pl|
... 1sg| X|  |  | X|  | X|  X|   |   |  X|
... 1pl| X|  |  | X|  | X|   |  X|  X|   |
... 2sg|  | X| X|  |  | X|  X|   |   |  X|
... 2pl|  | X| X|  |  | X|   |  X|  X|   |
... 3sg|  | X|  | X| X|  |  X|   |   |  X|
... 3pl|  | X|  | X| X|  |   |  X|  X|   |
... ''')
```

```pycon
>>> print(c)  # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
<Context object mapping 6 objects to 10 properties [b9d20179] at 0x...>
       |+1|-1|+2|-2|+3|-3|+sg|+pl|-sg|-pl|
    1sg|X |  |  |X |  |X |X  |   |   |X  |
    1pl|X |  |  |X |  |X |   |X  |X  |   |
    2sg|  |X |X |  |  |X |X  |   |   |X  |
    2pl|  |X |X |  |  |X |   |X  |X  |   |
    3sg|  |X |  |X |X |  |X  |   |   |X  |
    3pl|  |X |  |X |X |  |   |X  |X  |   |
```

```pycon
>>> c.objects
('1sg', '1pl', '2sg', '2pl', '3sg', '3pl')
```

```pycon
>>> c.properties
('+1', '-1', '+2', '-2', '+3', '-3', '+sg', '+pl', '-sg', '-pl')
```

```pycon
>>> c.bools  # doctest: +NORMALIZE_WHITESPACE
[(True, False, False, True, False, True, True, False, False, True),
 (True, False, False, True, False, True, False, True, True, False),
 (False, True, True, False, False, True, True, False, False, True),
 (False, True, True, False, False, True, False, True, True, False),
 (False, True, False, True, True, False, True, False, False, True),
 (False, True, False, True, True, False, False, True, True, False)]
```

```pycon
>>> c.intension(['1sg'])
('+1', '-2', '-3', '+sg', '-pl')
```

```pycon
>>> c.extension(['+1'])
('1sg', '1pl')
```

```pycon
>>> c.neighbors(['1sg', '1pl', '2pl'])
[(('1sg', '1pl', '2sg', '2pl', '3sg', '3pl'), ())]
```

```pycon
>>> c['1sg',]
(('1sg',), ('+1', '-2', '-3', '+sg', '-pl'))
```

```pycon
>>> c['1sg', '1pl', '2pl']
(('1sg', '1pl', '2sg', '2pl'), ('-3',))
```

```pycon
>>> c['-1', '-sg']
(('2pl', '3pl'), ('-1', '+pl', '-sg'))
```

```pycon
>>> print(c.relations())
+sg equivalent   -pl
+pl equivalent   -sg
+1  complement   -1
+2  complement   -2
+3  complement   -3
+sg complement   +pl
+sg complement   -sg
+pl complement   -pl
-sg complement   -pl
+1  incompatible +2
+1  incompatible +3
+2  incompatible +3
+1  implication  -2
+1  implication  -3
+2  implication  -1
+3  implication  -1
+2  implication  -3
+3  implication  -2
-1  subcontrary  -2
-1  subcontrary  -3
-2  subcontrary  -3
```

methods.name: concepts.definitions.Triple.take
documentation:
Return a subset with given objects/properties as new definition.

Args:

: objects: Iterable of object label strings.
  properties: Iterable of property label strings.
  reorder (bool):

Returns:

: Definition: A new {class}`.Definition` instance.

methods.name: concepts.definitions.Triple.transposed
documentation:
Return a new definition swapping `objects` and `properties`.

Returns:

: Definition: A new {class}`.Definition` instance.

methods.name: concepts.definitions.Triple.inverted
documentation:
Return a new definition flipping all booleans.

Returns:

: Definition: A new {class}`.Definition` instance.

Triple of `(objects, properties, bools)` for creating a context.

```pycon
>>> t = Triple(['Mr. Praline', 'parrot'], ['alive', 'dead'],
...     [(True, False), (False, True)])
```

```pycon
>>> t  # doctest: +NORMALIZE_WHITESPACE
<Triple(['Mr. Praline', 'parrot'], ['alive', 'dead'],
    [(True, False), (False, True)])>
```

```pycon
>>> print(t)
           |alive|dead|
Mr. Praline|X    |    |
parrot     |     |X   |
```

```pycon
>>> tuple(t)
(('Mr. Praline', 'parrot'), ('alive', 'dead'), [(True, False), (False, True)])
```

```pycon
>>> (t[0], t[1], t[2]) == (t.objects, t.properties, t.bools)
True
```

```pycon
>>> t == (t.objects, t.properties, t.bools)
True
```

```pycon
>>> t['Mr. Praline', 'alive']
True
```

```pycon
>>> t['parrot', 'alive']
False
```

```pycon
>>> t.take(['parrot'])
<Triple(['parrot'], ['alive', 'dead'], [(False, True)])>
```

```pycon
>>> t.take(properties=['dead'])
<Triple(['Mr. Praline', 'parrot'], ['dead'], [(False,), (True,)])>
```

```pycon
>>> t.take(['Brian'], ['alive', 'holy'])
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
KeyError: ['Brian', 'holy']
```

```pycon
>>> t.take(['parrot', 'Mr. Praline'], ['alive'], reorder=True)
<Triple(['parrot', 'Mr. Praline'], ['alive'], [(False,), (True,)])>
```

```pycon
>>> print(t.transposed())
     |Mr. Praline|parrot|
alive|X          |      |
dead |           |X     |
```

```pycon
>>> print(t.inverted())
           |alive|dead|
Mr. Praline|     |X   |
parrot     |X    |    |
```

Mutable triple of `(objects, properties, bools)` for creating a context.

Create definition from `objects`, `properties`, and `bools` correspondence.

Args:

: objects: Iterable of object label strings.
  properties: Iterable of property label strings.
  bools: Iterable of `len(objects)` tuples of `len(properties)` booleans.

Returns:

: Definition: New {class}`.Definition` instance.

Example:
: ```pycon
  >>> Definition(['man', 'woman'], ['male', 'female'], [(True, False), (False, True)])
  <Definition(['man', 'woman'], ['male', 'female'], [(True, False), (False, True)])>
  ```

Usage:

```pycon
>>> d = Definition()
```

```pycon
>>> d
<Definition([], [], [])>
```

```pycon
>>> d.add_object('King Arthur')
```

```pycon
>>> print(d)
           |
King Arthur|
```

```pycon
>>> d.add_object('Sir Robin', ['human', 'knight'])
>>> d.add_object('holy grail')
```

```pycon
>>> print(d)
           |human|knight|
King Arthur|     |      |
Sir Robin  |X    |X     |
holy grail |     |      |
```

```pycon
>>> d.add_object('King Arthur', ['human', 'knight', 'king'])
>>> d.add_property('mysterious', ['holy grail', 'Sir Robin'])
```

```pycon
>>> print(d)
           |human|knight|king|mysterious|
King Arthur|X    |X     |X   |          |
Sir Robin  |X    |X     |    |X         |
holy grail |     |      |    |X         |
```

```pycon
>>> d['Sir Robin', 'mysterious'] = False
```

```pycon
>>> print(d)
           |human|knight|king|mysterious|
King Arthur|X    |X     |X   |          |
Sir Robin  |X    |X     |    |          |
holy grail |     |      |    |X         |
```

```pycon
>>> e = d.copy()
>>> e.move_object('holy grail', 0)
>>> e.move_property('mysterious', 0)
>>> e.move_property('king', 1)
>>> print(e)
           |mysterious|king|human|knight|
holy grail |X         |    |     |      |
King Arthur|          |X   |X    |X     |
Sir Robin  |          |    |X    |X     |
```

```pycon
>>> e = d.copy()
>>> e.rename_object('Sir Robin', 'Launcelot')
>>> e.add_property('brave', ['Launcelot'])
>>> e.rename_object('holy grail', 'grail')
>>> e.rename_property('mysterious', 'holy')
```

```pycon
>>> print(e)
           |human|knight|king|holy|brave|
King Arthur|X    |X     |X   |    |     |
Launcelot  |X    |X     |    |    |X    |
grail      |     |      |    |X   |     |
```

```pycon
>>> print(e | d)
           |human|knight|king|holy|brave|mysterious|
King Arthur|X    |X     |X   |    |     |          |
Launcelot  |X    |X     |    |    |X    |          |
grail      |     |      |    |X   |     |          |
Sir Robin  |X    |X     |    |    |     |          |
holy grail |     |      |    |    |     |X         |
```

```pycon
>>> print(e & d)
           |human|knight|king|
King Arthur|X    |X     |X   |
```

```pycon
>>> e.remove_object('grail')
>>> e.remove_property('holy')
>>> e.rename_object('King Arthur', 'Arthur')
>>> e.set_property('king', [])
>>> e.set_object('Launcelot', ['human'])
```

```pycon
>>> print(e)
         |human|knight|king|brave|
Arthur   |X    |X     |    |     |
Launcelot|X    |      |    |     |
```

```pycon
>>> e.set_property('knight', ['Launcelot'])
```

```pycon
>>> print(e)
         |human|knight|king|brave|
Arthur   |X    |      |    |     |
Launcelot|X    |X     |    |     |
```

modules.name: concepts.lattices
documentation: Formal Concept Analysis concept lattices.
lattices.py - build FCA concepts lattice from context

classes.name: concepts.lattices.Lattice

methods.name: concepts.lattices.Lattice.__init__
documentation: Create lattice from context.

documentation: Annotate object/attribute concepts with their objects/properties.

documentation: Pickle lattice as `(context, concepts)` tuple.

methods.name: concepts.lattices.Lattice._eq
documentation:
Return `True` if two lattices are equivalent.

Notes:

: Does not compares their context objects.
  Lattice-equivalence comparison is present mainly for unit-tests
  (not meant to be efficient). Context-comparison should be superior
  in most cases.

Return concept having all given `properties` as intension.

: {class}`.Concept` instances from this lattice.

: {class}`.Concept` instances from this lattice.

methods.name: concepts.lattices.Lattice.graphviz
documentation:
Return DOT source for visualizing the lattice graph.

Args:

: filename: Path to the DOT source file for the Digraph.
  directory: (Sub)directory for DOT source saving and rendering.
  render (bool): Call `.render()` on the result.
  view (bool): Call `.render(view=True)` on the result.
  make_object_label: Callable with iterable of objects argument

  > returning a string to be used as object label.

  make_property_label: Callable with iterable of properties argument

  : returning a string to be used as object label.

Returns:

: A `graphviz.Digraph` instance.

Formal concept lattice as directed acyclic graph of concepts.

Usage:

```pycon
>>> from concepts import contexts
```

```pycon
>>> l = contexts.Context.fromstring('''
...    |+1|-1|+2|-2|+3|-3|+sg|+pl|-sg|-pl|
... 1sg| X|  |  | X|  | X|  X|   |   |  X|
... 1pl| X|  |  | X|  | X|   |  X|  X|   |
... 2sg|  | X| X|  |  | X|  X|   |   |  X|
... 2pl|  | X| X|  |  | X|   |  X|  X|   |
... 3sg|  | X|  | X| X|  |  X|   |   |  X|
... 3pl|  | X|  | X| X|  |   |  X|  X|   |
... ''').lattice
```

```pycon
>>> print(l)  # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
<Lattice object of 6 atoms 22 concepts 5 coatoms at 0x...>
    {} <-> [+1 -1 +2 -2 +3 -3 +sg +pl -sg -pl]
    {1sg} <-> [+1 -2 -3 +sg -pl] <=> 1sg
    {1pl} <-> [+1 -2 -3 +pl -sg] <=> 1pl
    {2sg} <-> [-1 +2 -3 +sg -pl] <=> 2sg
    {2pl} <-> [-1 +2 -3 +pl -sg] <=> 2pl
    {3sg} <-> [-1 -2 +3 +sg -pl] <=> 3sg
    {3pl} <-> [-1 -2 +3 +pl -sg] <=> 3pl
    {1sg, 1pl} <-> [+1 -2 -3] <=> +1
    {1sg, 2sg} <-> [-3 +sg -pl]
    {1sg, 3sg} <-> [-2 +sg -pl]
    {1pl, 2pl} <-> [-3 +pl -sg]
    {1pl, 3pl} <-> [-2 +pl -sg]
    {2sg, 2pl} <-> [-1 +2 -3] <=> +2
    {2sg, 3sg} <-> [-1 +sg -pl]
    {2pl, 3pl} <-> [-1 +pl -sg]
    {3sg, 3pl} <-> [-1 -2 +3] <=> +3
    {1sg, 2sg, 3sg} <-> [+sg -pl] <=> +sg -pl
    {1pl, 2pl, 3pl} <-> [+pl -sg] <=> +pl -sg
    {1sg, 1pl, 2sg, 2pl} <-> [-3] <=> -3
    {1sg, 1pl, 3sg, 3pl} <-> [-2] <=> -2
    {2sg, 2pl, 3sg, 3pl} <-> [-1] <=> -1
    {1sg, 1pl, 2sg, 2pl, 3sg, 3pl} <-> []
```

```pycon
>>> l.infimum
<Infimum {} <-> [+1 -1 +2 -2 +3 -3 +sg +pl -sg -pl]>
```

```pycon
>>> l.supremum
<Supremum {1sg, 1pl, 2sg, 2pl, 3sg, 3pl} <-> []>
```

```pycon
>>> l.atoms  # doctest: +NORMALIZE_WHITESPACE
(<Atom {1sg} <-> [+1 -2 -3 +sg -pl] <=> 1sg>,
 <Atom {1pl} <-> [+1 -2 -3 +pl -sg] <=> 1pl>,
 <Atom {2sg} <-> [-1 +2 -3 +sg -pl] <=> 2sg>,
 <Atom {2pl} <-> [-1 +2 -3 +pl -sg] <=> 2pl>,
 <Atom {3sg} <-> [-1 -2 +3 +sg -pl] <=> 3sg>,
 <Atom {3pl} <-> [-1 -2 +3 +pl -sg] <=> 3pl>)
```

```pycon
>>> l['1sg', '1pl', '2pl']
<Concept {1sg, 1pl, 2sg, 2pl} <-> [-3] <=> -3>
```

```pycon
>>> l['-1', '-sg']
<Concept {2pl, 3pl} <-> [-1 +pl -sg]>
```

```pycon
>>> l(['+1', '-sg'])
<Atom {1pl} <-> [+1 -2 -3 +pl -sg] <=> 1pl>
```

```pycon
>>> l.join([l['1sg',], l['1pl',], l['2sg',]])
<Concept {1sg, 1pl, 2sg, 2pl} <-> [-3] <=> -3>
```

```pycon
>>> l.join([])
<Infimum {} <-> [+1 -1 +2 -2 +3 -3 +sg +pl -sg -pl]>
```

```pycon
>>> l.meet([l['-1',], l['-2',], l['-pl',]])
<Atom {3sg} <-> [-1 -2 +3 +sg -pl] <=> 3sg>
```

```pycon
>>> l.meet([])
<Supremum {1sg, 1pl, 2sg, 2pl, 3sg, 3pl} <-> []>
```

```pycon
>>> l = contexts.Context.fromstring('''
...    |+1|-1|+2|-2|+3|-3|+sg|+du|+pl|-sg|-du|-pl|
... 1s | X|  |  | X|  | X|  X|   |   |   |  X|  X|
... 1de| X|  |  | X|  | X|   |  X|   |  X|   |  X|
... 1pe| X|  |  | X|  | X|   |   |  X|  X|  X|   |
... 1di| X|  | X|  |  | X|   |  X|   |  X|   |  X|
... 1pi| X|  | X|  |  | X|   |   |  X|  X|  X|   |
... 2s |  | X| X|  |  | X|  X|   |   |   |  X|  X|
... 2d |  | X| X|  |  | X|   |  X|   |  X|   |  X|
... 2p |  | X| X|  |  | X|   |   |  X|  X|  X|   |
... 3s |  | X|  | X| X|  |  X|   |   |   |  X|  X|
... 3d |  | X|  | X| X|  |   |  X|   |  X|   |  X|
... 3p |  | X|  | X| X|  |   |   |  X|  X|  X|   |
... ''').lattice
```

```pycon
>>> l  # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
<Lattice object of 11 atoms 65 concepts 6 coatoms at 0x...>
```

classes.name: concepts.lattices.Concept

methods.name: concepts.lattices.Concept.__iter__
documentation: Yield `extent` and `intent` (e.g. for pair unpacking).

methods.name: concepts.lattices.Concept.extent
documentation: tuple[str, ...] The objects subsumed by the concept.

methods.name: concepts.lattices.Concept.intent
documentation: tuple[str, ...] The properties implied by the concept.

methods.name: concepts.lattices.Concept.minimal
documentation:
Shortlex minimal properties generating the concept.

Returns:
    tuple[str, ...]: Property name strings.

methods.name: concepts.lattices.Concept.attributes
documentation:
Yield properties generating the concept in shortlex order.

Yields:
    Tuples of property name strings.

methods.name: concepts.lattices.Concept.upset
documentation:
Yield implied concepts including `self`.

Yields:

: {class}`.Concept` instances.

methods.name: concepts.lattices.Concept.downset
documentation:
Yield subsumed concepts including `self`.

Yields:

: {class}`.Concept` instances.

methods.name: concepts.lattices.Concept.implies
documentation:
Implication comparison.

Args:

: other (Concept): {class}`.Concept` instance from the same lattice.

Returns:

: bool: `True` if `self` implies `other` else `False`.

methods.name: concepts.lattices.Concept.subsumes
documentation:
Subsumption comparison.

Args:

: other (Concept): {class}`.Concept` instance from the same lattice.

Returns:

: bool: `True` if `self` subsumes `other` else `False`.

methods.name: concepts.lattices.Concept.properly_implies
documentation:
Proper implication comparison.

Args:

: other (Concept): {class}`.Concept` instance from the same lattice.

Returns:

: bool: `True` if `self` properly implies `other` else `False`.

methods.name: concepts.lattices.Concept.properly_subsumes
documentation:
Proper subsumption comparison.

Args:

: other (Concept): {class}`.Concept` instance from the same lattice.

Returns:

: bool: `True` if `self` properly subsumes `other` else `False`.

methods.name: concepts.lattices.Concept.join
documentation:
Least upper bound, supremum, or, generalization.

Args:

: other (Concept): {class}`.Concept` instance from the same lattice.

Returns:

: Concept: {class}`.Concept` instance from the same lattice.

methods.name: concepts.lattices.Concept.meet
documentation:
Greatest lower bound, infimum, and, unification.

Args:

: other (Concept): {class}`.Concept` instance from the same lattice.

Returns:

: Concept: {class}`.Concept` instance from the same lattice.

methods.name: concepts.lattices.Concept.incompatible_with
documentation:
Infimum meet comparison.

Args:

: other (Concept): {class}`.Concept` instance from the same lattice.

Returns:

: bool: `True` if `self` is incompatible with `other` else `False`.

methods.name: concepts.lattices.Concept.complement_of
documentation:
Infimum meet and supremum join comparison.

Args:

: other (Concept): {class}`.Concept` instance from the same lattice.

Returns:

: bool: `True` if `self` is the complement of `other` else `False`.

methods.name: concepts.lattices.Concept.subcontrary_with
documentation:
Non-infimum meet and supremum join comparison.

Args:

: other (Concept): {class}`.Concept` instance from the same lattice.

Returns:

: bool: `True` if `self` is the subcontrary to `other` else `False`.

methods.name: concepts.lattices.Concept.orthogonal_to
documentation:
Non-infimum meet, incomparable, and non-supremum join comparison.

Args:

: other (Concept): {class}`.Concept` instance from the same lattice.

Returns:

: bool: `True` if `self` is orthogonal to `other` else `False`.

Formal concept as pair of extent and intent.

Usage:

```pycon
>>> from concepts import contexts
```

```pycon
>>> l = contexts.Context.fromstring('''
...    |+1|-1|+2|-2|+3|-3|+sg|+pl|-sg|-pl|
... 1sg| X|  |  | X|  | X|  X|   |   |  X|
... 1pl| X|  |  | X|  | X|   |  X|  X|   |
... 2sg|  | X| X|  |  | X|  X|   |   |  X|
... 2pl|  | X| X|  |  | X|   |  X|  X|   |
... 3sg|  | X|  | X| X|  |  X|   |   |  X|
... 3pl|  | X|  | X| X|  |   |  X|  X|   |
... ''').lattice
```

```pycon
>>> c = l['+1',]
```

```pycon
>>> c
<Concept {1sg, 1pl} <-> [+1 -2 -3] <=> +1>
```

```pycon
>>> c.index, c.dindex
(7, 6)
```

```pycon
>>> c.extent
('1sg', '1pl')
```

```pycon
>>> c.intent
('+1', '-2', '-3')
```

```pycon
>>> c.objects
()
```

```pycon
>>> c.properties
('+1',)
```

```pycon
>>> c.atoms  # doctest: +NORMALIZE_WHITESPACE
(<Atom {1sg} <-> [+1 -2 -3 +sg -pl] <=> 1sg>,
 <Atom {1pl} <-> [+1 -2 -3 +pl -sg] <=> 1pl>)
```

```pycon
>>> c.minimal()
('+1',)
```

```pycon
>>> list(c.attributes())
[('+1',), ('+1', '-2'), ('+1', '-3'), ('-2', '-3'), ('+1', '-2', '-3')]
```

```pycon
>>> c.upper_neighbors  # doctest: +NORMALIZE_WHITESPACE
(<Concept {1sg, 1pl, 2sg, 2pl} <-> [-3] <=> -3>,
 <Concept {1sg, 1pl, 3sg, 3pl} <-> [-2] <=> -2>)
```

```pycon
>>> c.lower_neighbors  # doctest: +NORMALIZE_WHITESPACE
(<Atom {1sg} <-> [+1 -2 -3 +sg -pl] <=> 1sg>,
 <Atom {1pl} <-> [+1 -2 -3 +pl -sg] <=> 1pl>)
```

```pycon
>>> list(c.upset())  # doctest: +NORMALIZE_WHITESPACE
[<Concept {1sg, 1pl} <-> [+1 -2 -3] <=> +1>,
 <Concept {1sg, 1pl, 2sg, 2pl} <-> [-3] <=> -3>,
 <Concept {1sg, 1pl, 3sg, 3pl} <-> [-2] <=> -2>,
 <Supremum {1sg, 1pl, 2sg, 2pl, 3sg, 3pl} <-> []>]
```

```pycon
>>> list(c.downset())  # doctest: +NORMALIZE_WHITESPACE
[<Concept {1sg, 1pl} <-> [+1 -2 -3] <=> +1>,
 <Atom {1sg} <-> [+1 -2 -3 +sg -pl] <=> 1sg>,
 <Atom {1pl} <-> [+1 -2 -3 +pl -sg] <=> 1pl>,
 <Infimum {} <-> [+1 -1 +2 -2 +3 -3 +sg +pl -sg -pl]>]
```

```pycon
>>> l['+1',] <= l['-3',] <= l['-3',] <= l[()]
True
```

```pycon
>>> l['+1',] <= l['+sg',] or l['+sg',] <= l['+1',]
False
```

```pycon
>>> l['+1',] >= l['+1', '+sg'] >= l['+1', '+sg'] >= l['+1', '-1']
True
```

```pycon
>>> l['+1',] >= l['+sg',] or l['+sg',] >= l['+1',]
False
```

```pycon
>>> l['+1',] < l['-3',] < l[()]
True
```

```pycon
>>> l['+1',] > l['+1', '+sg'] > l['+1', '-1']
True
```

```pycon
>>> l['+1',] | l['+2',]
<Concept {1sg, 1pl, 2sg, 2pl} <-> [-3] <=> -3>
```

```pycon
>>> l['-1', '-2'] & l['-pl',]
<Atom {3sg} <-> [-1 -2 +3 +sg -pl] <=> 3sg>
```

```pycon
>>> l['+1',].incompatible_with(l['+3',])
True
```

```pycon
>>> l['+1',].incompatible_with(l['+sg',])
False
```

```pycon
>>> l['+1',].complement_of(l['-1',])
True
```

```pycon
>>> l['+1',].complement_of(l['+3',])
False
```

```pycon
>>> l['-1',].subcontrary_with(l['-3',])
True
```

```pycon
>>> l['-1',].subcontrary_with(l['+sg',])
False
```

```pycon
>>> l['+1',].orthogonal_to(l['+sg',])
True
```

```pycon
>>> l['+1',].orthogonal_to(l['+3',])
False
```

classes.name: concepts.lattices.Infimum

methods.name: concepts.lattices.Infimum.minimal
documentation:
Shortlex minimal properties generating the concept.

Returns:
    tuple[str, ...]: Property name strings.
Contradiction with empty `extent` and universal `intent`.

classes.name: concepts.lattices.Atom
documentation: Concept which is a minimal non-zero element in its lattice.

classes.name: concepts.lattices.Supremum
documentation: Tautology with universal `extent` and empty `intent`.
